fdaLabelSummary: Losartan can be administered once or twice daily with total daily doses ranging from 25 mg to 100 mg; 150 mg did not give greater effect than 50 to 100 mg.

notes: FDA label-normalized upper studied daily dose: doses up to 80 mg have been used, though greater effect above 40 mg is not shown.

name: Rosuvastatin
notes: FDA label-normalized dosage range: 5 mg to 40 mg once daily.
fdaLabelSummary: The dosage range is 5 to 40 mg orally once daily.

name: Pravastatin
notes: FDA label-normalized dosage range in adults.
fdaLabelSummary: Adults: recommended starting dosage is 40 mg to 80 mg once daily.

name: Lovastatin
notes: FDA label-normalized maximum daily dosage.
fdaLabelSummary: Lovastatin tablets are dosed up to 80 mg/day.

name: Pitavastatin
notes: FDA label-normalized maximum recommended dosage.
fdaLabelSummary: The maximum recommended dosage is 4 mg once daily.

name: Fluvastatin
notes: FDA label-normalized adult dosage range depending on formulation.
fdaLabelSummary: Adults: recommended starting dose is 40 mg to 80 mg daily; extended-release tablets are dosed at 80 mg once daily.

name: Enalapril
notes: FDA label-normalized maximum daily dose.
fdaLabelSummary: The dosage may be titrated upward until blood pressure is controlled or to a maximum of 40 mg daily.

name: Ramipril
notes: FDA label-normalized upper maintenance dose for hypertension.
fdaLabelSummary: The usual maintenance dose following titration is 2.5 mg to 20 mg daily as a single dose or equally divided doses.

name: Benazepril
notes: FDA label-normalized upper evaluated daily dose.
fdaLabelSummary: The studied dosing range is 10 to 80 mg; total daily doses above 80 mg have not been evaluated.

name: Captopril
notes: FDA label-normalized maximum daily dose.
fdaLabelSummary: A maximum daily dose of 450 mg captopril tablets should not be exceeded.

name: Fosinopril
notes: FDA label-normalized upper studied daily dose.
fdaLabelSummary: Once-daily doses of 20 mg to 80 mg were used in trials; some patients are unlikely to have a further response to 80 mg.

name: Moexipril
notes: FDA label-normalized upper daily range used in multiple-dose studies.
fdaLabelSummary: In multiple dose studies, moexipril hydrochloride was studied in the dose range of 7.5 to 30 mg once daily.

name: Perindopril
notes: FDA label-normalized maximum daily dose for hypertension.
fdaLabelSummary: The dosage may be titrated upward until blood pressure is controlled or to a maximum of 16 mg per day.

name: Quinapril
notes: FDA label-normalized upper daily dose.
fdaLabelSummary: Most patients have required dosages of 20, 40, or 80 mg/day, given as a single dose or in two equally divided doses.

name: Trandolapril
notes: FDA label-normalized adult dosage ceiling represented by the 4 mg tablet strength and labeled daily use.
fdaLabelSummary: Trandolapril tablets are labeled in strengths up to 4 mg for oral once-daily administration.

name: Irbesartan
notes: FDA label-normalized upper daily dose.
fdaLabelSummary: The recommended initial dose is 150 mg once daily; 300 mg once daily is the upper labeled daily dose.

name: Candesartan
notes: FDA label-normalized adult total daily dose range up to 32 mg/day.
fdaLabelSummary: Adult hypertension dosing ranges from 8 mg to 32 mg total daily dose.

name: Olmesartan
notes: FDA label-normalized adult maximum daily dose.
fdaLabelSummary: The dose may be increased to a maximum of 40 mg once daily.

name: Telmisartan
notes: FDA label-normalized daily dose range up to 80 mg once daily.
fdaLabelSummary: Hypertension dosing is 40 mg once daily, with a range of 40 to 80 mg once daily.

name: Eprosartan
notes: Deferred FDA normalization pending a clean current single-agent label source in this pass.
fdaLabelSummary: Current scalar is retained as a comparison placeholder until the single-agent FDA label reference is normalized.

name: Azilsartan
notes: FDA label-normalized recommended adult dose.
fdaLabelSummary: The recommended dose in adults is 80 mg taken orally once daily.

name: Atenolol
notes: FDA label-normalized usual adult dose up to 100 mg/day.
fdaLabelSummary: Atenolol is typically given as 50 mg once daily and may be increased to 100 mg once daily.

name: Bisoprolol
notes: FDA label-normalized daily dose range up to 20 mg once daily.
fdaLabelSummary: Bisoprolol fumarate is studied and labeled in doses up to 20 mg once daily.

name: Propranolol
notes: Deferred FDA normalization: propranolol labeling differs substantially by indication and formulation.
fdaLabelSummary: Propranolol oral dosing is highly indication-specific, with different labeled maxima across products and uses.

name: Nadolol
notes: FDA label-normalized daily dose range up to 320 mg once daily.
fdaLabelSummary: Doses up to 240 mg or 320 mg once daily may be needed.

name: Nebivolol
notes: FDA label-normalized adult dose range up to 40 mg once daily.
fdaLabelSummary: Nebivolol has been studied in dose ranges up to 40 mg once daily.

name: Labetalol
notes: FDA label-normalized upper daily dose used in severe hypertension: 2400 mg/day.
fdaLabelSummary: Patients with severe hypertension may require from 1200 mg to 2400 mg per day.

name: Acebutolol
notes: FDA label-normalized daily dose range up to 1200 mg/day.
fdaLabelSummary: Acebutolol dosing is generally 600 to 1200 mg per day after titration.

name: Betaxolol
notes: FDA label-normalized daily dose range up to 40 mg once daily.
fdaLabelSummary: Betaxolol doses of 20 mg and 40 mg once daily were studied.

name: Pindolol
notes: FDA label-normalized daily dose range up to 60 mg/day.
fdaLabelSummary: Divided dosages in the range of 10 mg to 60 mg daily have been shown to be effective.

name: Timolol
notes: Deferred FDA normalization pending a cleaner current oral timolol label source in this pass.
fdaLabelSummary: Current scalar is retained as a comparison placeholder until a current oral timolol label reference is normalized.

name: Sotalol
notes: Deferred FDA normalization: sotalol labeling is strongly indication-specific and safety-constrained by QT monitoring.
fdaLabelSummary: Sotalol dosing differs materially by arrhythmia indication and renal function.

name: Diltiazem
notes: FDA label-normalized extended-release daily dose range up to 540 mg/day.
fdaLabelSummary: Diltiazem extended-release capsules are studied in doses ranging from 120 mg to 540 mg once daily.

name: Verapamil
notes: FDA label-normalized extended-release daily dose up to 480 mg/day.
fdaLabelSummary: Extended-release verapamil labeling includes doses up to 480 mg/day.

name: Nifedipine
notes: FDA label-normalized extended-release daily dose up to 120 mg/day.
fdaLabelSummary: Nifedipine extended-release products are labeled up to 120 mg/day.

name: Nicardipine
notes: FDA label-normalized oral capsule daily dose up to 120 mg/day.
fdaLabelSummary: Nicardipine capsules are labeled in oral dosing up to 120 mg/day.

name: Nisoldipine
notes: FDA label-normalized extended-release daily dose up to 40 mg/day.
fdaLabelSummary: Nisoldipine extended-release tablets are available up to 40 mg once daily.

name: Felodipine
notes: FDA label-normalized extended-release daily dose up to 20 mg/day.
fdaLabelSummary: Felodipine extended-release tablets are labeled up to 20 mg once daily.

name: Isradipine
notes: Deferred FDA normalization pending a cleaner current oral isradipine label pass.
fdaLabelSummary: Current scalar is retained as a comparison placeholder until the current oral isradipine label is normalized.

name: Clevidipine
notes: FDA labeling describes short-term infusion experience up to 32 mg/hour rather than a universal daily maximum.
fdaLabelSummary: Cleviprex infusion is titrated up to a maximum dose of 32 mg/hour, with limited short-term experience at that level.

name: Citalopram
notes: FDA label-normalized maximum dose; higher doses are not recommended because of QT risk.
fdaLabelSummary: Dosages above 40 mg once daily are not recommended due to the risk of QT prolongation.

name: Paroxetine
notes: Deferred FDA normalization: labeled maximum varies by formulation and indication, so this scalar is retained temporarily as a comparison placeholder.
fdaLabelSummary: Paroxetine maxima vary by product and indication, including lower caps for some formulations and up to 60 mg/day for some indications.

name: Fluvoxamine
notes: FDA label-normalized maximum adult daily dose.
fdaLabelSummary: Adult maximum dose is 300 mg/day.

name: Aripiprazole
notes: FDA label-normalized adult maximum dose.
fdaLabelSummary: Adult aripiprazole tablet labeling lists a maximum dose of 30 mg/day.

name: Olanzapine
notes: Deferred FDA normalization pending a cleaner current oral olanzapine label pass.
fdaLabelSummary: Current scalar is retained as a comparison placeholder until a current oral olanzapine label source is normalized.

name: Risperidone
notes: FDA label-normalized adult maximum dose.
fdaLabelSummary: Adult risperidone dosing is titrated within a range up to 16 mg/day.

name: Ziprasidone

fdaLabelSummary: Iloperidone tablet labeling includes doses up to 24 mg/day.

notes: FDA label-normalized adult maximum dose.
fdaLabelSummary: Clozapine labeling includes titration up to a maximum of 900 mg/day.

name: Alprazolam
notes: Deferred FDA normalization: labeled maxima differ by indication and formulation, including higher panic-disorder trial doses.
fdaLabelSummary: The maximum recommended dosage is 4 mg daily for anxiety indications, while panic-disorder trials included doses in the range of 1 mg to 10 mg daily.

name: Diazepam
notes: Deferred FDA normalization: labeled dosing varies substantially by indication and schedule.
fdaLabelSummary: Adult daily dosing varies from 2 mg to 10 mg given 2 to 4 times daily depending on indication.

name: Lorazepam
notes: Deferred FDA normalization: labeled dosing varies by indication and schedule, so the scalar is retained as a comparison placeholder.
fdaLabelSummary: The usual dosage range is 2 mg to 6 mg daily in divided doses, with higher total daily doses used in some patients.

name: Temazepam
notes: FDA label-normalized usual adult dose up to 30 mg at bedtime.
fdaLabelSummary: The recommended usual adult dose is 15 mg before retiring; some patients may need 30 mg.

name: Oxazepam
notes: Deferred FDA normalization: labeled total daily dose depends on anxiety severity and divided-dose schedule.
fdaLabelSummary: Usual doses range from 10 to 15 mg three or four times daily for milder anxiety and 15 to 30 mg three or four times daily for more severe syndromes.

name: Chlordiazepoxide
notes: Deferred FDA normalization: labeled daily dosing varies by anxiety versus alcohol withdrawal indication.
fdaLabelSummary: Adult dosing includes up to 300 mg per day for alcohol-withdrawal management in the oral label.

name: Clorazepate
notes: Deferred FDA normalization: labeled daily dosing differs by indication and titration schedule.
fdaLabelSummary: For alcohol-withdrawal management, labeled oral dosing starts at 30 mg and may continue at 30 to 60 mg in divided doses.

name: Midazolam
notes: Deferred FDA normalization: available oral labeling is pediatric syrup and weight-based, not a single universal adult oral max.
fdaLabelSummary: Oral midazolam syrup labeling uses mg/kg dosing up to a maximum dose of 40 mg in pediatric use.

name: Triazolam
notes: FDA label-normalized maximum recommended dosage.
fdaLabelSummary: The maximum recommended dosage is 0.5 mg once daily.

name: Estazolam
notes: FDA label-normalized usual upper adult dose.
fdaLabelSummary: Most adults use 1 mg before bedtime; a 2 mg dose may be used when clinically necessary.

name: Flurazepam
notes: FDA label-normalized adult bedtime dose up to 30 mg.
fdaLabelSummary: Flurazepam hydrochloride capsules are labeled in 15 mg and 30 mg strengths for bedtime use.

name: Aspirin
notes: Antiplatelet-oriented scalar retained for comparison, but this entry is not yet normalized to a dedicated FDA aspirin labeling source.

name: Clopidogrel
notes: FDA label-normalized maintenance dose.
fdaLabelSummary: 75 mg once daily orally.

name: Prasugrel
notes: FDA label-normalized maintenance dose for most adults.
fdaLabelSummary: Continue at 10 mg once daily.

name: Ticagrelor

name: Dipyridamole
notes: FDA label-normalized scheduled daily total.
fdaLabelSummary: 75-100 mg four times daily.

name: Cilostazol
notes: FDA label-normalized scheduled daily total.
fdaLabelSummary: 100 mg twice daily.

name: Abciximab
notes: FDA labeling is weight-based; stored scalar reflects the label's infusion cap rather than a universal daily maximum.
fdaLabelSummary: 0.25 mg/kg IV bolus, then 0.125 mcg/kg/min infusion to a maximum of 10 mcg/min for 12 hours.

name: Eptifibatide
notes: FDA labeling is weight- and renal-function-based; stored scalar is retained only as a comparison placeholder.
fdaLabelSummary: 180 mcg/kg IV bolus, second 180 mcg/kg bolus after 10 minutes, then 2 mcg/kg/min infusion; 1 mcg/kg/min if CrCl less than 50 mL/min.

name: Tirofiban
notes: FDA labeling is weight- and renal-function-based; stored scalar is retained only as a comparison placeholder.
fdaLabelSummary: 25 mcg/kg IV over 3 minutes, then 0.15 mcg/kg/min infusion; 0.075 mcg/kg/min if renal impairment applies.

name: Vorapaxar
notes: FDA label-normalized scheduled daily total.
fdaLabelSummary: 2.08 mg orally once daily.

notes: DailyMed standard adult tablet regimens use 875 mg every 12 hours or 500 mg every 8 hours based on the amoxicillin component.

name: Penicillin V Potassium
notes: DailyMed adult oral regimens commonly use 250 mg to 500 mg every 6 to 8 hours.

name: Dicloxacillin
notes: DailyMed adult oral regimens commonly use 250 mg every 6 hours on an empty stomach.

name: Cephalexin
notes: DailyMed adult oral dosage ranges from 1 g to 4 g daily in divided doses.

name: Cefadroxil
notes: DailyMed adult oral regimens use 1 g to 2 g per day in a single dose or divided twice daily.

name: Cefuroxime Axetil
notes: DailyMed adult oral regimens commonly use 250 mg or 500 mg every 12 hours.

name: Cefdinir
notes: DailyMed adult oral regimens use 300 mg every 12 hours or 600 mg once daily.

name: Cefpodoxime
notes: DailyMed adult oral regimens commonly use 100 mg to 400 mg every 12 hours depending on infection type.

name: Cefixime
notes: DailyMed adult oral regimens use 400 mg once daily or 200 mg every 12 hours.

name: Azithromycin
notes: DailyMed common adult tablet regimens use 500 mg once daily for 3 days or 500 mg on day 1 followed by 250 mg daily.

name: Clarithromycin
notes: DailyMed adult tablet regimens commonly use 250 mg or 500 mg every 12 hours.

name: Erythromycin
notes: DailyMed usual adult oral dosing uses one 250 mg tablet four times daily or one 500 mg tablet every 12 hours.

name: Doxycycline
notes: DailyMed adult oral dosing commonly uses 200 mg on day 1 as 100 mg every 12 hours, followed by 100 mg daily; selected infections use 100 mg every 12 hours.

name: Minocycline
notes: DailyMed adult oral dosing commonly uses 200 mg initially followed by 100 mg every 12 hours.

name: Clindamycin
notes: DailyMed adult oral regimens use 150 mg to 300 mg every 6 hours for serious infections and 300 mg to 450 mg every 6 hours for more severe infections.

name: Nitrofurantoin
notes: DailyMed adult oral monohydrate/macrocrystal regimens use one 100 mg capsule every 12 hours for seven days.

name: Ciprofloxacin
notes: DailyMed adult oral regimens commonly use 250 mg to 750 mg every 12 hours depending on infection type.

name: Levofloxacin
notes: DailyMed adult oral regimens commonly use 250 mg, 500 mg, or 750 mg every 24 hours depending on infection type.

name: Fosfomycin
notes: DailyMed adult oral dosing uses a single 3 g sachet dose for uncomplicated urinary tract infection.
